meta.siteDescription: Licensed nurses for post-operative recovery, elderly daily care, wound care, injections, and IV therapy — delivered to your home in Beirut and Mount Lebanon.

footer.blurb: Licensed home nursing care in {area}. Founded and clinically supervised by a Lebanese BSN.

hero.headline: Hospital-trained nurses, sent
hero.sub: Lebanese RNs and PNs working their hospital off-days. Coordinated by WhatsApp. A written summary in the family portal after every visit.

categories.heading: Hospital-grade care, at home
categories.subtitle: Clinical visits are performed by licensed RNs. Companion shifts are staffed by trained junior caregivers, supervised by our lead nurse. You get the right level of support — no more, no less.
categories.viewAll: See the full list of services

postOp.title: Post-Operative Care
postOp.description: Recover safely at home after surgery with nurses who follow your discharge plan.

elderly.title: Elderly Daily Care
elderly.description: Consistent, dignified support for elderly patients living at home.

ivWound.title: Injections, Wounds & IV
ivWound.description: Clinical procedures done at home — no hospital trip required.

junior.title: Companion & Junior Care
junior.description: Trained junior caregivers for non-clinical support, supervised by a lead RN.

howItWorks.headline: Three moments.
howItWorks.lede: A coordinator replies on WhatsApp, a nurse visits, a written summary lands in the family portal the same day. No phone tag.

steps.headline: Message us — in your own words.
steps.body: WhatsApp is the front door. A few lines is enough. EN · AR · FR. A coordinator replies the same day with what the visit needs.

steps.headline: A licensed nurse arrives — matched to the case.
steps.body: RN or PN, depending on the procedure. Same-nurse continuity when the case is ongoing.

steps.headline: A written summary, the same day.
steps.body: Vitals, what was done, what to watch for, next visit. In the family portal — visible to whoever you share it with.

serviceArea.headline: Beirut
serviceArea.lede: Districts where the coordinator currently has nurses available. If your district isn't listed, message us anyway — most off-map cases find a fit.
serviceArea.offMapBody: Message the coordinator anyway — most off-map cases find a fit.

ctaBanner.heading: Ready to talk to a nurse?
ctaBanner.subtitle: Message us with the patient's situation. Free 15-minute phone consult — no obligation, no hard sell.

diaspora.headline: Your parents are in Beirut.
diaspora.lede: Pay from Paris. Read the visit summary in Dubai. Approve the next visit from London. The portal works in three timezones at once.

services.headline: Care that comes
services.lede: Five services, plus a sixth for cases that don't fit a category. Every visit is matched by clinical need, not by package. No prices on the public site — quote on WhatsApp.

items.label: 01 · Clinical visits
items.headline: Injections, IV insertion, hydration, wound care.
items.body: IM/SubQ injection, IV start and medication administration, Foley insertion, complex dressing changes.

items.label: 02 · Recovery at home
items.headline: Post-surgical care after discharge.
items.body: Wound, drain, mobility, hand-off coordination with the operating team.

items.label: 03 · Daily care
items.headline: Daily care for an elder parent.

items.label: 04 · Bloodwork at home
items.headline: Routine, fasting, same-day samples.
items.body: Collected at home and walked to the lab.

catchAll.label: — Outside this list?
catchAll.headline: Not every case fits a category.
catchAll.body: Message the coordinator. Most off-list cases find a fit.

faq.headline: Questions, before we
faq.lede: If your question isn't here, the coordinator will answer it on WhatsApp the same day.
faq.helperBody: Message the coordinator — most replies land within a few hours during Beirut daytime.

items.question: Why don't you publish prices?
items.answer: High-touch service. Every case has a different shape. We quote on WhatsApp after a short conversation.

items.question: How does payment from abroad work?
items.answer: Western Union, OMT International, Whish Money, or direct USD wire. The coordinator sends you a summary of what's billed and how to pay in your timezone.

items.question: Can I read the visit summary in Arabic?
items.answer: Yes. The portal has EN, AR, FR. The summary is written in the language the coordinator and you agreed on.

items.question: What if it's 3am where I am?
items.answer: The coordinator replies in your daytime. The portal is always available. Emergencies still get same-day response in Beirut time.

items.question: How are the nurses vetted?
items.answer: Lebanese Order of Nurses licensed, currently employed at AUBMC, Hôtel-Dieu, or St Georges. Off-day shifts only. Background-checked.

items.question: Can I keep the same nurse week to week?
items.answer: Yes, when the case is ongoing. Same-nurse continuity is the default for daily care and recovery cases.

items.question: What ends up in the family portal?
items.answer: Vitals, what was done that visit, what to watch for, next visit date. Wound photos in a private bucket if applicable.

items.question: What's not offered yet?
items.answer: Live-in care, NG tube, ICU/tracheostomy specialty, Holter monitoring, oxygen rental. Coming later.

meta.title: Packages — Caregivers Collective
meta.description: Three ways to be billed for home nursing in Beirut & Mt. Lebanon — single visit, shift block, or monthly wellness subscription. Quote on WhatsApp.

hero.headline: Three shapes
hero.sub: Single visit, shift block, or monthly subscription. We pick the shape that fits the case — not the other way around. No prices on the public site; every quote is custom.

modes.headline: The visit, the shift,
modes.lede: Each shape has a different cadence. Same nurses, same documentation, same coordinator — only the way it's billed changes. Quotes happen on WhatsApp.

items.label: The visit
items.headline: Per-visit, one thing at a time.
items.body: Best when the case is procedural and bounded — a single injection, a wound dressing change, an IV start. We come, do the work, send the summary.
bullets: IM/SubQ injection, IV insertion, Foley, complex dressings
Single nurse, one written summary per visit
Quote based on procedure, time, and supplies
items.ctaMessage: Hi — I'd like to discuss a per-visit care quote.

items.label: The shift
items.headline: 8, 12, or 24-hour blocks.
items.body: Most common shape. For recovery from surgery, ongoing daily care, or overnight presence. The same nurse covers a continuous shift — RN or PN matched to the case.
bullets: RN or PN — matched to the clinical need
Written summary at end of each shift
items.ctaMessage: Hi — I'd like to discuss shift-based care for a family member.

items.label: The wellness rhythm
items.headline: Monthly check-ins for an elder parent.
items.body: A subscription cadence — four check-in visits per month, family portal access, escalation path when something changes. For families abroad who want a steady presence in the home.
bullets: Four visits per month, same coordinator
Priority WhatsApp response from the coordinator
items.ctaMessage: Hi — I'd like to discuss the monthly wellness subscription.

faq.headline: Pricing questions
faq.lede: Five honest answers before you ask the coordinator.
faq.helperBody: Message the coordinator with what you need — most quotes come back the same day.

items.question: Why don't you publish prices?
items.answer: High-touch service. Every case has a different shape — procedure, duration, nurse level, supplies, distance. We quote on WhatsApp after a short conversation.

items.question: How does payment from abroad work?
items.answer: Western Union, OMT International, Whish Money, or direct USD wire. The coordinator sends you a summary of what's billed and how to pay in your timezone.

items.question: Are supplies (drugs, IV bags, dressings) included?
items.answer: Standard supplies for the visit are included in most quotes. Specialty drugs or large dressings are quoted separately — the coordinator will be explicit.

items.question: Do I commit to a shape upfront?
items.answer: No. Many families start with a single visit, then move to shifts or a monthly cadence once the case stabilises. The coordinator helps you decide.

items.question: Can I switch between shapes mid-month?
items.answer: Yes. Pricing adjusts the day the shape changes — no penalty. The coordinator confirms the new cadence in writing on WhatsApp.

items.answer: Yes. The portal has EN, AR, FR. The summary is written in the language the coordinator and you agreed on.

items.answer: The coordinator replies in your daytime. The portal is always available. Emergencies still get same-day response in Beirut time.

items.answer: Lebanese Order of Nurses licensed, currently employed at AUBMC, Hôtel-Dieu, or St Georges. Off-day shifts only. Background-checked.

items.question: Can I keep the same nurse week to week?
items.answer: Yes, when the case is ongoing. Same-nurse continuity is the default for daily care and recovery cases.

items.question: What if my district isn't in your list?
items.answer: Message the coordinator anyway. Most off-map cases find a fit — we can usually match a nurse who lives or works nearby.

items.question: Do you offer emergency visits at 3am Beirut time?
items.answer: Same-day response is our standard. Overnight emergency response depends on nurse availability for that night — message the coordinator and we'll tell you immediately.

items.question: Can the coordinator speak with my parent's doctor?
items.answer: Yes, when authorised. With consent, the coordinator can hand off vitals or visit summaries to the treating physician's office.

meta.title: For nurses — Caregivers Collective
meta.description: Lebanese RNs and PNs: take home-care shifts on your off-days. Independent service provider, no hospital conflict, fair rates, written reviews.

hero.headline: Off-day shifts,
hero.sub: If you're already a licensed RN or PN at AUBMC, Hôtel-Dieu, St Georges, or similar — we coordinate home-care shifts for your off-days. Independent service provider, no hospital conflict, fair rates.

steps.headline: Three steps.
steps.lede: Apply, get matched to cases that fit your schedule, get paid weekly. You decide which shifts to take. We never compete with your hospital schedule.

steps.headline: Apply on WhatsApp.
steps.body: Send your license number, current hospital, and the off-days you're typically free. The coordinator runs a background check and confirms your availability.

steps.headline: Get matched to a case.
steps.body: When a case fits your skills and schedule, the coordinator sends you the details on WhatsApp — case shape, address, visit duration, expected supplies. You accept or decline.

steps.headline: Get paid weekly.
steps.body: Submit your visit summary in the portal at end of shift. Pay lands weekly via Whish, OMT, or direct bank. The family pays Caregivers Collective; Caregivers Collective pays you.

benefits.headline: Built for nurses
benefits.lede: We respect that you have a primary hospital. Every part of this is designed to work around that — not against it.

items.label: Schedule
items.title: Off-day only.
items.body: You tell us your off-days each month. We never offer cases that conflict with your hospital schedule. The platform respects your primary employer.

items.label: Status
items.title: Independent service provider.
items.body: Not an employee. Each shift is a service contract. We handle the billing, the family relationship, the documentation — you do the clinical work.

items.label: Reviews
items.title: Written, not anonymous.
items.body: After each visit, the coordinator (not the family) writes a short review. Reviews are part of your record on the platform — visible to you, used for case matching.

items.label: Rates
items.title: Fair, published to you.
items.body: Rates are quoted to you upfront before you accept the case. RN rates differ from PN rates. Overnight and weekend cases carry a premium. No surprises.

faq.headline: Common questions
faq.lede: If yours isn't here, message the coordinator on WhatsApp.
faq.helperBody: Send your license number, current hospital, and off-days on WhatsApp. The coordinator replies within a day or two.

items.question: Do I need to be currently employed at a hospital?
items.answer: Yes — the platform is designed for nurses with primary hospital employment. AUBMC, Hôtel-Dieu, St Georges are the most common; similar hospitals work too.

items.question: Will my hospital find out?
items.answer: No. We coordinate around your hospital schedule, not with your hospital. Nothing about your primary employment is shared. You declare your off-days; we offer cases that fit them.

items.question: How am I paid?
items.answer: Weekly. The family pays Caregivers Collective for the case; Caregivers Collective pays you the agreed rate via Whish, OMT, or direct bank. Rates are confirmed before you accept each case.

items.question: What if a case goes wrong?
items.answer: Document what you saw in the portal. The coordinator handles the family conversation. We have professional liability coverage on platform-coordinated visits.

items.question: Can I decline cases?
items.answer: Always. You see case details before you accept. No penalty for declining — your acceptance rate matters less than your reliability after accepting.

items.question: What's a typical shift like?
items.answer: Visit shapes vary: a 90-minute injection visit, a 12-hour overnight recovery shift, a 24-hour daily-care block. The case offer tells you everything before you accept.

meta.title: Contact — Caregivers Collective
meta.description: Reach the coordinator on WhatsApp, by phone, or by message. Beirut & Mount Lebanon home nursing — EN / AR / FR.

hero.headline: Talk to the
hero.sub: WhatsApp is the fastest path. Phone for emergencies. The form below is for when neither works.

whatsapp.headline: Open the chat —
whatsapp.lede: WhatsApp is the easiest way to start. Tell the coordinator what's happening in your own words. Most replies land within a few hours during Beirut daytime.

form.headline: If WhatsApp isn't
form.lede: The form lands in the coordinator's inbox. Replies happen on email or WhatsApp — whichever you prefer.

formFields.name: Your name
formFields.messagePlaceholder: Tell us about the patient — current condition, what kind of help is needed, when.
formFields.successBody: We'll reply within 30 minutes between 7am and 11pm. Overnight messages get a reply by 7am.
